description: This figure characterizes the deformation microstructure of hydrogen-charged austenitic Fe30Mn6.5Al0.3C low-density steel after room temperature tensile testing, investigating hydrogen's effect on Type 2 dislocation structure. Subfigure (a) is an inverse pole figure (IPF) EBSD map with 10 μm scale bar, showing macroscopic deformation bands (DB1, DB2, DB3) formed in a grain oriented close to [1 1 18] parallel to the tensile axis (TA). Subfigure (b) is the magnified EBSD map of the marked area in (a) with 5 μm scale bar, focusing on DB1 with orientation [2 1 15]//TA, with <110>{111} slip system marked. Subfigures (c-e) are electron channeling contrast imaging (ECCI) micrographs with 1 μm, 500 nm, 250 nm scale bars respectively, revealing fine dislocation structures inside DB1: microbands (MBs, 150-200 nm thick, spaced 2-3 μm along {111} slip planes) and equiaxed dislocation cells (DCs). This figure confirms hydrogen promotes multi-scale strain localization, forming macroscopic DBs and microscopic MBs/DCs, supporting the conclusion that hydrogen-enhanced microbanding improves strain hardening and hydrogen embrittlement resistance of the studied steel.
